mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings. This positive fundamental outlook drives investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate significantly above 1.0% signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, increasing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers. This dampens economic activity, compresses corporate profit margins, and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High and growing employment (PAYEMS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicates a strong labor market and healthy consumer confidence, fueling both consumption and investment. This synergistic effect drives corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Periods where both PAYEMS and HOUST show sustained growth do not result in statistically significant outperformance of the S&P 500 over 20-day periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signifies genuine economic expansion and productivity gains without overheating. This fundamental strength supports corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL is consistently below 2.5% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and a decline in corporate earnings expectations. This triggers immediate negative sentiment and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive or flat (within +/- 0.1%) following more than 60% of instances where UNRATE increases by > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) provides an environment for stable consumer purchasing power and allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policies. This reduces discount rates and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL readings below 2.0% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a strong economy with supportive monetary conditions. This combination fosters business expansion and investment, leading to strong corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0.0%
failure_condition: Periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% and FEDFUNDS is stable or declining do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This indicates a potential economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1000.0 (thousands of units) for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1000.0 for two consecutive months.
